coredata.dc:title: One-pot hydrothermal green synthesis of Polygala tenuifolia mediated graphene quantum dots for acetylcholine esterase inhibitory activity
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Drug Delivery Science and Technology
coredata.dc:creator: Tak, Kajal
Sharma, Prashansa
Sharma, Rekha
Dave, Vivek
Jain, Smita
Sharma, Swapnil
coredata.dc:description: The present paper reports the synthesis of Donepezil hydrochloride functionalized Graphene quantum dots (DO-ptGQD) for the cure of Alzheimer disease. Graphene quantum dots were synthesized through root extract of Polygala tenuifolia by using one-pot hydrothermal synthesis method. Using UV/Visible spectroscopy, FTIR, SEM, TEM and XPS methods, the synthesized ptGQD was evaluated. Further the formulated DO-ptGQD was evaluated by SEM, TEM, FTIR, XPS techniques and was further evaluated for the encapsulation study, in-vitro study and various in-vivo models. Through behavioural model (Radial arm maze and Water morris maze assay) the learning and memory ability of the formulations was evaluated and the outcomes indicate that the DO-ptGQD markedly decreases the transmission latency to get in the baited arm in 12.19 ± 0.39 s or to the hidden stage in 15.37 ± 0.45 in 7 days. DO-ptGQD show more percentage inhibition of AchE enzyme that is 86.23 ± 1.28 as likened with the drug and ptGQD. DO-ptGQD considerably increases the protein and glutathione level and decreases the nitric oxide and lipid peroxide level. The Histopathological image indicates that DO-ptGQD treats the disorganization of the cell and show more preservation of small pyramidal cells as compared with the other formulations. In conclusion, DO-ptGQD was safe and effective drug delivery system for the treatment of Alzheimer disease and also the root extract of Polygala tenuifolia provide synergistic effect for the treatment of disease.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Polygala tenuifolia
Graphene quantum dots
Donepezil hydrochloride
Alzheimer disease
Acetyl cholinesterase
Histopathological study

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Journal of Drug Delivery Science and Technology

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Functionalization of ptGQD with donepezil hydrochloride (DO-ptGQD)
Particle size, PDI and zeta potential analysis
Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM)
Inhibition of acetyl cholinesterase enzyme assay (AchE)
Estimation of brain lipid peroxides content
Estimation of brain nitric oxide content
Particle size, polydispersity index (PDI) and zeta potential
Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM)
Inhibition of acetyl cholinesterase enzyme assay (AchE)
Estimation of brain lipid peroxides content
Estimation of brain nitric oxide content

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) FESEM image of the synthesized ptGQD (B) FESEM image of DO-ptGQD (C) TEM image of the synthesized ptGQD (D) TEM image of DO-ptGQD.
ATR-FTIR spectra of A) ptGQD B) Donepezil hydrochloride C) DO-ptGQD.
XPS spectra of A) ptGQD B) DO-ptGQD.

(A) Scopolamine-induced AD significantly increased the transfer latency to reach the baited arm (p < 0.001) as compared to the control group. However, Post treatment with ptGQD and DO-ptGQD significantly reduced the transfer latency compared to donepezil treated group (p < 0.001) and control group (p < 0.001), respectively. (B) Scopolamine-induced AD significantly increased the time duration to reach the platform (p < 0.001) as compared to the normal group. However, Post treatment with ptGQD and DO-ptGQD significantly reduced the transfer latency compared to donepezil treated group (p < 0.001) and control group (p < 0.001), respectively. Data shown is a mean of n = 6 replicates ± S.D., ***p < 0.001.

Depicts effect of different treatments, which measured as acetylcholinesterase (AchE) levels in hippocampus homogenate. The AchE level was significantly higher in scopolamine -treated rats as compared to the donepezil group and showed zero percent inhibition of enzyme. Post treatment with ptGQD and DO-ptGQD significantly increased the percentage inhibition of AchE when compared with scopolamine group to be similar to donepezil group. Data shown is a mean of n = 6 replicates ± S.D., ***p < 0.001.

(A, B, D) Depicts effect of different treatments on GSH, tissue protein, nitric oxide (NO) which measured as GSH levels, tissue protein levels and nitric oxide levels in hippocampus homogenate. The GSH, tissue protein and nitric oxide content expressed as micromole/g tissue, mg/mL and micromole respectively were significantly lower in scopolamine-treated rats as compared to the donepezil group. Post treatment with ptGQD and DO-ptGQD significantly increased GSH, tissue protein and nitric oxide (p < 0.05) when compared scopolamine group and restored GSH, tissue protein and nitric oxide brain levels to be similar to donepezil group. Data shown is a mean of n = 6 replicates ± S.D., ***p < 0.001. (C) Depicts effect of different treatments, which measured as thiobarbituric acid substances (TBARs) levels in hippocampus homogenate. The TBARs level was significantly higher in scopolamine -treated rats as compared to the scopolamine group and showed zero percent inhibition of enzyme. Post treatment with ptGQD and DO-ptGQD significantly increased the percentage inhibition of TBARs when compared with scopolamine group to be similar to donepezil group. Data shown is a mean of n = 6 replicates ± S.D., ***p < 0.001.
Histopathological study of hippocampus tissue of rat A) Control B) Positive control group C) Donepezil treated group D) ptGQD treated group E) DO-ptGQD treated group {Pyramidal cell, Vaculolation }.
Formulations ptGQD, particle size (nm), polydispersity index (PDI) and zeta potential (mV) of the synthesized ptGQDs and DO-ptGQD.
Effect of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD on anti-alzheimer activity using radial arm maze assay.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: All values were expressed as mean ± SD (n = 6).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Effect of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD on anti-alzheimer activity using water morris maze assay.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: All values were expressed as mean ± SD (n = 6).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Effect of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD on AchE, GSH, TBARS, Protein carbonyl NO activity in hippocampus tissue of rats.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: All values were expressed as mean ± SD (n = 6).

head.ce:title: One-pot hydrothermal green synthesis of Polygala tenuifolia mediated graphene quantum dots for acetylcholine esterase inhibitory activity

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Pharmacy, Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan, India
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Pharmacy, Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan, India

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Home Science, Mahila Mahavidyala, Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, India
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Home Science, Mahila Mahavidyala, Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, India

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan, India
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Chemistry, Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan, India

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Pharmacy, School of Health Science, Central University of South Bihar, India
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Pharmacy, School of Health Science, Central University of South Bihar, India

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author. Department of Pharmacy, School of Health Science, Central University of South Bihar, Bihar, 824236, India.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The present paper reports the synthesis of Donepezil hydrochloride functionalized Graphene quantum dots (DO-ptGQD) for the cure of Alzheimer disease. Graphene quantum dots were synthesized through root extract of Polygala tenuifolia by using one-pot hydrothermal synthesis method. Using UV/Visible spectroscopy, FTIR, SEM, TEM and XPS methods, the synthesized ptGQD was evaluated. Further the formulated DO-ptGQD was evaluated by SEM, TEM, FTIR, XPS techniques and was further evaluated for the encapsulation study, in-vitro study and various in-vivo models. Through behavioural model (Radial arm maze and Water morris maze assay) the learning and memory ability of the formulations was evaluated and the outcomes indicate that the DO-ptGQD markedly decreases the transmission latency to get in the baited arm in 12.19 ± 0.39 s or to the hidden stage in 15.37 ± 0.45 in 7 days. DO-ptGQD show more percentage inhibition of AchE enzyme that is 86.23 ± 1.28 as likened with the drug and ptGQD. DO-ptGQD considerably increases the protein and glutathione level and decreases the nitric oxide and lipid peroxide level. The Histopathological image indicates that DO-ptGQD treats the disorganization of the cell and show more preservation of small pyramidal cells as compared with the other formulations. In conclusion, DO-ptGQD was safe and effective drug delivery system for the treatment of Alzheimer disease and also the root extract of Polygala tenuifolia provide synergistic effect for the treatment of disease.

ce:section.ce:para: Alzheimer disease is a type of neurodegenerative disorder and a main cause of dementia [1]. The disease occurs due to the various causes, but the major cause is the low level of acetylcholine neurotransmitter in the brain due to the rapid hydrolysis through acetylcholinesterase enzyme [2,3]. Globally the alzheimer disease is the top 10 cause of the death in the elder publics and it was reported that from 2000 to 2030 the occurrence of disease gets increased from 420 million to about 1 billion persons and the manifestation frequency of the ailment rises with rise in the age [4,5]. Donepezil, a most prescribed medication used to inhibit Acetylcholine esterase (AchE) enzyme and thus increase the acetylcholine level in the brain. It provides a long term inhibition of the cholinesterase enzyme in the brain, and also provides a protective effect on neuroinflammation, observed in Alzheimer disease [6,7]. However due to gastrointestinal side effects, its therapeutic effect gets limited and also due to repetitive uptake of 5 mg dose daily cause various complications particularly amid Alzheimer patients who have severe dementia. Moreover donepezil gets widely distributed in all tissues and metabolized rapidly [8]. In the brain capillaries, Blood Brain Barrier (BBB) is present, composed of tight and compact junctions forming the major barrier for the transport of active drug particles from the vascular area to the brain tissue [9,10]. The current challenge is to design such a drug delivery system that allows the delivery of drug through BBB in a safe and effective manner.

However in the field of nanomedicine, Quantum dots based drug delivery system has been seeking attention in the management of various neurodegenerative disorders such as Alzheimer disease, Parkinson disease etc. [11] Quantum dots are commonly the example of nanotechnology, they act as drug carrier for the transport of drug into the brain as they easily permeate BBB because of their small size, high solubility and large surface area. Quantum dots are the nanometer scale semiconductor crystals through various composites like Cd, Pb, Ag, Zn etc. [12,13].

From the several kinds of quantum dots such as silicon dots, cadmium quantum dots, silver selenide dot etc. Graphene quantum dots have many benefits such as more water solubility, less toxicity, steady fluorescence properties, enhanced bioavailability etc. [14,15] Up to now, various methods have been utilized to synthesize graphene quantum dots [16], among them thermal treatment such as microwave assisted synthesis and one-pot hydrothermal synthesis method, being commonly utilized. For the fabrication of GQDs, carbon precursor is classified under two categories such as chemical and biological [17]. The chemical precursor involves the use of harsh chemicals and method of purification is very complex. Due to this limitations, biological or natural precursor are used which are cheap, synthesis method is ecofriendly, large scale production, non-toxic and thus leads to the green-synthesis based method for fabricating GQDs. For the synthesis of graphene quantum dots, various natural precursors were used such as plant materials [18], milk [19], some harmful bacteria [20], and food waste [21] etc. act as excellent source of carbon.

In this Research work, a green synthesis method was employed for the synthesis of graphene quantum dots (ptGQD) using root extract of Polygala tenuifolia. Polygala tenuifolia is a herb in the family of Polygalaceae. Polygala root contains many beneficial neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory compounds including Tenuifolisides, 3, 6-Disinapoylsucrose and saponins. The roots extract mainly used in the improvement of memory, improve cognitive functions in elderly, combat depression etc. [22–24] After the synthesis of ptGQD they were functionalized with donepezil hydrochloride. By formulating this type of drug carrier system we aimed to increase the acetylcholine level in the brain. The root extract of Polygala tenuifolia will provide synergistic effect for the treatment of alzheimer disease.

Root extract of Polygala tenuifolia was purchased from Alibaba, China. Donepezil hydrochloride was purchased from Cipla Pharmaceutical Ltd. Scopolamine hydrochloride, EDC (1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethyl amino propyl carbodimide), NHS (N-hydroxy succinimide), trichloroacetic acid (TCA), sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), Thiobarbituric acid (TBA), in addition remaining compounds were obtained from Sigma-Aldrich, USA.

GQDs were synthesized by using One-pot hydrothermal synthesis method. In this method, briefly 250 mg of root extract was weighed and mixed with 10 ml of water, then sonicate the above mixture for 15 min. Afterwards the mixture was transferred in Teflon lined autoclave reactor. The hydrothermal reaction was carried out at different-different temperature as shown in Table 1 . After the completion of reaction, the mixture present in the autoclave reactor is filtered. Then centrifuge the obtained filtrate at 28000g speed for 15 min, so as to obtain clear supernatant [18,25]. Then the obtained ptGQD solution was dialyzed using filter (cut off 3.5 kDa) for 72 h with replacement of water in every 6 h. After the complete dialysis process, the ptGQD solution was dried in hot air oven at 60°c for 24 h to obtain pure ptGQD.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Functionalization of ptGQD with donepezil hydrochloride (DO-ptGQD)
ce:section.ce:para: The optimized ptGQD was functionalized with Donepezil hydrochloride via coupling the amino group of donepezil with the carboxylic group of ptGQD. In this method, 3 mg of EDC and 4 mg of NHS were added in 10 ml of the ptGQD solution. The mixture was kept on vigorous stirring on magnetic stirrer. Then add 10 mg of Donepezil hydrochloride into the above solution with continuous stirring for 2 h [26,27]. After that, obtained solution was dialyzed using filter (cut off 3.5 kDa) for 72 h with replacement of water in every 6 h, so as to obtain pure DO-ptGQD.

The synthesized graphene quantum dot was confirmed through Lab India 3000+ Uv–visible spectrophotometer equipped through software UvWin. The spectrum was recorded in the wavelength of 200 nm–800 nm. Further, for background absorption HPLC water was employed as a blank [28].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Particle size, PDI and zeta potential analysis
ce:section.ce:para: For the determination of average dots size and PDI of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD, zeta sizer (Malvern nano ZS instrument) was used. To determine, sample solutions were distributed in HPLC water and the temperature of apparatus was fixed at 25 °C. Measurement of zeta potential determines the charge present on the surface of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD and it was determined from malvern zeta [29]. Dots with maximum or minimum positive zeta potential are typically reflected as electrophoretically stable. The testing was implemented six times and outcome was stated as ±SD.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM)
ce:section.ce:para: The surface morphology of the samples ptGQD and DO-ptGQD was studied with SEM analysis. Through Field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM) equipped through Mira 3 TESCAN software, SEM image of the samples were obtained [30].

Through TEM (MNIT Jaipur) the size and surface microscopy of the samples ptGQD and DO-ptGQD was studied [31]. To perform it, freeze-dried quantum dots were dispersed in HPLC water to obtain 0.1% w/v solution and formerly place one drop of the above solution on copper treated grid-iron and then dehydrate the solution and afterwards with 2% w/v phosphotungustic acid stain the solution. Dual grids were prepared for each sample and then viewed at different magnification power.

The FTIR spectrum of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD were obtained with the help of BRUKER EQUINOX 55 spectrophotometer [32] functioned at room temperature with mercury cadmium detector. The spectrum was recorded in the wavelength 4000-400 cm−1.

XPS study helps to define the chemical composition of ptGQD and also the change in the chemical composition after the functionalization of the donepezil hydrochloride. XPS was accomplished through ESCA Ulvac-PHI 1600 spectrometer [33].

To measure the actual quantity of drug loaded in the formulation, firstly the formulation was centrifuged to obtain a clear supernatant. Afterwards the supernatant was witnessed in UV/Vis spectrophotometer using HPLC water as a reference. The percentage encapsulation efficiency of donepezil hydrochloride in DO-ptGQD was determined through given formula
EE% = Amount of donepezil hydrochloride in formulation/ Amount of total loaded drug × 100

In this method, 5 ml formulation was put in dialysis bag of molecular weight cutoff 14,000 KD. The dialysis bag was placed in the beaker containing Phosphate buffer saline solution as a release media. The beaker was kept on magnetic stirrer with constant stirring at about 100 rpm with sustained temperature at 37 °C. Afterwards at a particular interval i.e. 1, 2, 3 … 72 h, 1 ml of aliquot was withdrawn and was substituted by the similar volume of fresh buffer to keep the sink state [35]. Formerly the sample was viewed in Uv–visible spectrophotometer (Labindia).

Male adult wistar rats of 220 gm – 250 gm weightiness were used for alzheimer activity. The experimental methods were legalized with prior consent from Institutional animal ethics committee with practice number BV/3770/2018–2019, besides the whole methods remained implemented in agreement through CPCSEA recommendations.

The working memory index of the animal was calculated through RAM assay. The maze consist of 8 arms prolonged as an octagonally designed of central core of diameter 30 cm. The stage was elevated exceeding 40 cm from the base. In the one arm food pellets were located and the rat was positioned in the central core, then rat transmission latency to get in the baited arm was noted. The animals were skilled for 14 days and later animals were categorized into 5 groups with 6 animals in every group. Group I considered as control group, Group II as positive control group (0.3 mg/kg), Group III animal receives donepezil (3 mg/kg), Group IV animals receives ptGQD (3 mg/kg) and Group V animals receive DO-ptGQD (3 mg/kg). For the stimulation of alzheimer disease scopolamine was given to all groups not including the control group [36,37]. Then the treatment was given after 60 min of induction to Group III, Group IV and Group V animals for 7 days. Formerly the similar process was continual and the animal transmission latency to get in the baited arm was noted.

The maze consist of enormous spherical tank consist of dark impervious polyvinylchloride with height 0.4 m–0.6 m and diameter 1.8 m–2.0 m and it was occupied with water up to the deepness of 0.3 m–0.4 m. A hidden stage was placed in tank which is 2 cm above from the water level. Afterwards the animal was drop in tank and then through automated video tracking system linked with microcomputer, the transmission latency to extent the hidden stage was noted. The animals were skilled for 14 days and later animals were categorized into 5 groups with 6 animals in every group. Group I considered as control group, Group II as positive control group, Group III animal receives donepezil, Group IV animals receives ptGQD and Group V animals receive DO-ptGQD. For the stimulation of alzheimer disease scopolamine was given to all groups not including the control group [27,38]. Then the treatment was given after 60 min of induction to Group III, Group IV and Group V animals for 7 days. Formerly the similar process was continued and the animal transmission latency to extent the hidden stage was noted.

The biochemical examinations were accomplished after the last behavioural model. Animal were sacrificed and brain was detached. The hippocampus was separated from the brain and in the sodium phosphate buffer solution it was homogenized. Later centrifuge the mixture and supernatant was used for various assay. .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Inhibition of acetyl cholinesterase enzyme assay (AchE)
ce:section.ce:para:
Through Uv–visible spectrophotometer, acetyl cholinesterase enzyme inhibition activity was measured through the process defined by Ellman. In AD, AchE enzyme hydrolyze the acetyl choline into thiocholine and it gets combine with Ellman reagent (DTNB) and form 5-thio-2 nitrobenzoate and 2-nitrobenzoate-5-mercaptothiocholine which is simply recognized at 412 nm in Uv–visible spectrophotometer. To accomplish the test, initially in the test tube take 10 μL of 6.67 UmL-1 AchE and then add 1710 μL of 50 mM sodium phosphate buffer, 10 μL of 200 mM acetyl thiocholine iodide, 20 μL of 10 mM Ellman reagent and 250 μL of ptGQD from the concentration ranging from 2 to 4 mM. Repeat the same procedure for standard donepezil solution and DO-ptGQD [39,40]. Later absorbance of all samples was taken at 412 nm in each 10 s for 3 min. The percentage enzyme inhibition was calculated through stated formula-
% inhibition = 100-change in sample absorbance/blank sample absorbance∗100

The experiment was implemented six times and then by plotting the graph amid % inhibitions against concentration of formulation, the IC50 value of the formulations to inhibit the hydrolysis of acetylcholine was determined.

With the help of Ellman method41 the level of reduced glutathione was determined. To determine it 1 ml of tissue homogenate of the control group, positive control group, donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD was taken in different-different test tubes. Now in all the test tubes add 5% of TCA for the precipitation of the proteins. After that the mixture was centrifuged to achieve a clear supernatant, and then add 3 ml of phosphate buffer and 0.5 ml of DTNB reagent to the supernatant [42]. Then the absorbance of sample was taken at 412 nm with appropriate blank sample which only contains TCA. The experiments were performed six times.

With the help of Lowry method [43], the level of tissue protein was estimated. For this 0.1 ml of tissue homogenate of the control group, positive control group, donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD was taken in different-different test tubes. Then in all the test tubes, add 0.9 ml water and 4.5 ml alkaline copper reagent. Place the above solution for 10 min at room temperature. Afterwards add Folin's reagent (0.5 ml) and after 20 min the absorbance of the sample was measured at 640 nm with the help of UV/Vis spectrophotometer. The protein level was stated as mg/g of tissue.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Estimation of brain lipid peroxides content
ce:section.ce:para: To determine the amount of lipid peroxide formed in the brain, a modified thiobarbituric acid reactive species (TBARS) assay was performed. To perform it firstly 0.2 ml of tissue homogenate of donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD groups was taken in different-different test tubes. Then in all the test tubes add SDS (8.1%), 20% acetic acid (1.5 ml), 0.8% TBA (1.5 ml). Then make up the volume up to 4 ml with distilled water. Afterwards the mixture was warmed at 95 °C for 60 min. After the complete cooling of mixture, add water (1 ml) and n-butanol/pyridine mixture (5 ml) and then the mixture was shaken robustly [44]. Then centrifuge the mixture at 4000 rpm for 10 min, separate the organic layer from mixture and then observed in UV/visible spectrophotometer at 532 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Estimation of brain nitric oxide content
ce:section.ce:para: With the help of Griess IlIosvoy method, scavenging activity of nitric oxide was determined. To estimate nitric oxide, sodium nitropruside (5 mM) in phosphate buffer saline was incubated with 1 ml of tissue homogenate from donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD groups in different test tubes for 60 min. After 60 min of incubation equal amount of Griess reagent was added and again incubated for 30 min in the dark and the absorbance of the samples were taken at 546 nm in UV/Vis spectrophotometer [45].

To perform the Histopathological study, cut the hippocampus into the width of 4 μM and in 10% formalin solution it was stored. Eosin and Hematoxylin dye was used for staining, then observed the sample at 450X magnification power in the microscope [46].

The attained outcomes were shown as means ± SD. Through one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) data was evaluated and through software Graph Pad Prism 5.0 all arithmetical analysis was accomplished. The Statistical significance was set at p < 0.05.

Absorption spectrum of the synthesized graphene quantum displayed, a distinct surface Plasmon's resonance group highlighted at 340 nm (Fig. 1 ), corresponding to the dark brown color solution of quantum dots, indicating the formation of ptGQD. The UV absorption peak at 340 nm occurs due to the n-π* electronic transition occurs within the carbon structure. This transition shows the presence of CC bond, nitrogen and oxygen comprising functional groups in the structure [47].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Particle size, polydispersity index (PDI) and zeta potential
ce:section.ce:para: The mean particle size diameter of the ptGQD was ranged from 16.20 ± 0.19 nm to 76.43 ± 0.25 nm and PDI value ranged from 0.218 ± 0.12 to 0.483 ± 0.36. The optimized graphene quantum dots showed particle size diameter 16.20 ± 0.19 nm and PDI value 0.483 ± 0.36. The result shows that the hydrothermal treatment of plant material (250 mg) at 300 °C for 8 h show a less particle size indicating that the this temperature is sufficient to carbonize the plant material into a dots of spherical shape and small size, Lower temperature (below 300 °C) would not sufficient to carbonize the plant material. On the other hand, higher temperature (above 300 °C) destroys the surface of the dots and yields the dots of larger size [18]. The zeta potential shows the stability of the synthesized graphene quantum dots ptGQD. The zeta potential values of all the dots were ranged from −16.10 ± 1.5 mV to −37.93 ± 0.93 mV [25]. The optimized formulation shows zeta potential more away from zero indicating the more stability of the formulation as compared to the all other formulation. The negative value indicates that the plant component contains negative charge which stabilizes the positively charged graphene quantum dots. Zeta potential value also suggests that the hydrothermal treatment of plant material (250 mg) at 300 °C for 8 h shows more stability as compared to all other temperatures. The results of particle size, PDI and zeta potential were shown in Table 1. On the basis of particle size, PDI and zeta potential, ptGQD 5 was the optimized formulation. Then the optimized formulation ptGQD 5 was functionalized with donepezil hydrochloride, and the particle size was found to be 82.13 ± 1.45 nm, PDI was 0.272 ± 0.05 and zeta potential was +40.2 ± 0.10 mV. The positive zeta potential value shows the entrapment of drug in the quantum dots [48]. This result indicates that the formulation will cross the BBB easily and will effectively treat the alzheimer disease and the plant Polygala tenuifolia itself has a potential to enhance the learning and memory capacity.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM)
ce:section.ce:para: SEM was performed to determine the surface morphology of the synthesized ptGQD and DO-ptGQD. The SEM image of ptGQD 5 (Fig. 2 (A)) indicates that the quantum dots were sphere-shaped and size was 10 nm. The SEM image (Fig. 2(B)) after the functionalization of donepezil hydrochloride (DO-ptGQD) shows that the dots were sphere-shaped and are very intense due to the loading of the drug. The size of the particles was found to be 80.45 nm which is concordance with the results observed by nano zeta sizer. Further the results were justified by the TEM analysis which has been discussed in later sections.

The TEM image of the ptGQD 5 (Fig. 2(C)) indicates that the dots were sphere-shaped and size was found to be 10 nm [49]. The quantum dots prepared with the hydrothermal treatment of extract of Polygala tenuifolia at different-different temperature. The hydrothermal temperature shows an important role in carbonizing the initial material through pyrolysis, as temperature lower than 300 °C would not adequate to carbonize the plant material to the graphene quantum dots and temperature higher than 300 °C would abolish their surface and structure. The TEM image of the DO-ptGQD shows that the particles were sphere-shaped and particles size was 80 nm (Fig. 2(D)). The intense particle indicates the incorporation of donepezil hydrochloride into the graphene quantum dots.

The FTIR spectra (Fig. 3 ) of ptGQD exhibited the widening of numerous peaks associated to oxygen containing functional groups i.e. at 3332.26 cm−1 (O–H streching vibration), 2114.52 cm−1 (due to the presence of C–H streching mode, 1636.12 cm−1 (due to the presence of aromatic CC skeletal ring vibration) [50]. The existence of the hydroxyl, carboxyl groups in the ptGQD gives excellent water solubility and stability for many biomedical applications. The ATR-FTIR spectra of donepezil hydrochloride show major characteristic peak at 3668.05 cm−1 (O–H streching, free) 3223.00 cm−1 (N–H streching), 1694.11 cm−1 (CO streching vibration), 1588.82 cm−1 (CC streching vibration), 1496.60 cm−1 (C–H bending alkene), 1307.96 cm−1 (C–N streching vibration), 920.81 (CC bending, alkene) [51]. Formulation DO-ptGQD show major characteristic peaks at 3515.54 cm−1, 2869.53 cm−1, 2403.37 cm−1, 2122.14 cm−1, 1749.80 cm−1, 1695.31 cm−1, 1591.21 cm−1, 1450.63 cm−1, 1305.03 cm−1, 1176.83 cm−1, 1075.46 cm−1, 958.33 cm−1, 869.06 cm−1, 746.09 cm−1, 645.49 cm−1 respectively. The formulation DO-ptGQD show peaks in the very close range and any possible interaction between the drug and optimized formulation was not found, which means that both are compatible with each other and does not show any type of chemical interaction. The optimized formulation contains peak of drug and ptGQD which clearly shows that the entrapment of drug.

The XPS spectra (Fig. 4 (A)) of ptGQD show two nearby peaks at 284.02 eV and 288.16 eV which is chiefly accredited to the carboxyl and graphitic functionalized C1s [52]. After deconvolution, CC group is presented at 284.5 eV binding energy, which is the characteristic peak of graphene's graphitic carbon. Furthermore, numerous peaks comprising C–O (286.54 eV), CO (289.12 eV) and COOH (288.16 eV) groups were detected in C 1s deconvoluted peak. The deconvolution of O 1s too displays C–O peaks, which confirms the carboxylic group's existence on ptGQD. After the surface modification of ptGQD with the donepezil hydrochloride the XPS spectrum was changed (Fig. 4(B)), an additional peak of N–H peak at 288.61 eV, N–CO at 285.67 eV, and C–N peak at 284.92 eV. The comparison of spectra of ptGQD and DO-ptGQD indicates that alteration in carbon type from graphitic and carboxyl groups (CC, C–O, CO, and –COOH) to graphitic and amide groups (CC, C–N, CO, and –N–CO), indicates the covalent bond development among the –COOH group of ptGQD and amine group of donepezil hydrochloride [26].

ce:list-item.ce:para: % encapsulation efficiency determine by the centrifugation method, to know the amount of drug present in the graphene quantum dots. The Encapsulation of the drug was found to be 85.50 ± 1.77.

ce:section.ce:para: The percentage cumulative amount of drug release (%CDR) of donepezil hydrochloride was obtained by the in-vitro drug release study [49]. The in-vitro drug release is methods adopt to mimic the in-vivo performance of the drug by maintaining the physiological condition, so that the pattern of drug release can be studied. The % CDR studies were performed with the function of time (hr) as 0, 1, 4, 6, 10, 18, 24, 48 and 72 h and the sampling was done by maintaining the sink condition with the phosphate buffer saline 1% Tween 80 (PBST), pH 7.4. The Formulation DO-ptGQD shows the % CDR of 83.27 ± 0.62.

The transmission latency of every animal to get in the baited arm was noted, with the help of Radial arm maze assay [37]. Treatment of rats by donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD considerably lowers the transmission latency by 25.42 ± 1.85 s, 22.36 ± 0.32 s and 12.19 ± 0.39 s respectively in 7 days. As the days were increasing the transmission latency in Group III, Group IV and Group V animals was reducing. The ptGQD decrease the transmission latency only up to 22.36 ± 0.32 s whereas DO-ptGQD decreases the transmission latency up to 12.19 ± 0.39 s (Table 2 and Fig. 5 (A)). These results evidently suggest that the DO-ptGQD was more efficient as compared with pure donepezil and ptGQD and thus DO-ptGQD effectively treat the alzheimer disease.

Through WMM assay, transmission latency to reach the hidden stage was noted [38]. Treatment of rats by donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD, considerably decreases the transmission latency by 30.31 ± 1.21 s, 25.60 ± 0.32 s and 15.37 ± 0.45 s respectively in 7 days. As the days were increasing the transmission latency in Group III, Group IV and Group V animals was reducing. The ptGQD decrease the transmission latency only up to 25.60 ± 0.32 s whereas DO-ptGQD decreases the transmission latency up to 15.37 ± 0.45 (Table 3 and Fig. 5(B)). These results evidently suggest that DO-ptGQD will improve the learning and memory of rats effectively as likened with the donepezil and ptGQD.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Inhibition of acetyl cholinesterase enzyme assay (AchE)

ce:section.ce:para: In AD the level of GSH was decreased due to which various free radical generate, that will cause more degeneration of neurons. The rats treated with the donepezil increase the GSH level up to 15.26 ± 0.86 whereas rats treated with ptGQD increase the GSH level up to 18.15 ± 1.74 and DO-ptGQD increases the GSH level up to 21.86 ± 0.82 (Fig. 7 (A) and Table 4). Increased level of GSH helps in the detoxification of the reactive oxygen species due to which free radicals will not generate and the neurons will not damage in AD [42]. Thus from the results obtained, it can be concluded that DO-ptGQD show more increase in the GSH level because it contains donepezil hydrochloride and the quantum dots of Polygala tenuifolia root extract which is known to increase the learning and memory ability, thus the drug and quantum dots in DO-ptGQD shows synergistic effect to increase the GSH level.

Rats treated with Scopolamine shows protein level of 10.26 ± 1.28 whereas rats treated with the donepezil increase the protein level up to 65.12 ± 2.14, rats treated with the ptGQD increase the protein level up to 73.19 ± 0.43 and rats treated with the DO-ptGQD increases the protein level up to 80.64 ± 1.38 (Fig. 7(B) and Table 4). Thus from the results obtained, it can be concluded that DO-ptGQD shows more increase in the protein level as compared to the pure donepezil and ptGQD because of the presence of both drug and quantum dots in the DO-ptGQD.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Estimation of brain lipid peroxides content
ce:section.ce:para: Lipid peroxides were evaluated as thiobarbituric acid reactive substances (TBARS). In alzheimer disease, the level of TBARS gets increased which damages the neurons. The result obtained from this study indicates that donepezil inhibits the TBARS up to 75.26 ± 0.85%, ptGQD inhibits the TBARS percentage up to 82.92 ± 2.25% and DO-ptGQD inhibits the TBARS percentage up to 87.36 ± 0.69 respectively (Fig. 7(C) and Table 4). TBARS increases the production of free radical and this free radical damages the tissue by attacking the membrane through peroxidation of unsaturated fatty acids [44]. The percentage inhibition of TBARS was more in DO-ptGQD, because of the presence of the both donepezil and ptGQD which will provide synergistic effect in inhibiting the TBARS and the plant Polygala tenuifolia possess antioxidant property which helps in the inhibition of TBARS.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Estimation of brain nitric oxide content
ce:section.ce:para: The level of nitric oxide was high in the group treated with the scopolamine i.e. 46.34 ± 1.32. The treatment Group III (Donepezil), Group IV (ptGQD) and Group V (DO-ptGQD) show a decreased level of nitric oxide i.e. 20.46 ± 0.12, 13.52 ± 1.86 and 8.64 ± 2.47 respectively (Fig. 7(D) and Table 4). Increased level of nitric oxide cause the production of free radical and thus damages the brain tissue. The level of nitric oxide was decreased by donepezil, ptGQD and DO-ptGQD; but more decrease in nitric oxide level was observed in the DO-ptGQD because of the presence of both donepezil and plant Polygala tenuifolia which provide synergistic effects.

Examination of brain segment of Group I (control) rats shows the hippocampus normal structure with dense layers of small pyramidal cells with vesicular nuclei and also showed few layers of vast pyramidal cells. On the other hand, the brain section of rats treated with scopolamine show disorganization and damage to small pyramidal cells, with contraction of enormous pyramidal cell and nuclei gets darken [46]. Group III (donepezil) rats illustrate conservation of small pyramidal cells with adequate inhibition of cell disorganization and vaculolation, however Group IV (ptGQD) show preservation of minor pyramidal cells with a minor amount of decline in the cell disorganization and vaculolation and Group V (DO-ptGQD) rats show a clear preservation of minor pyramidal cells with greater decline in cell disorganization and vaculolation (Fig. 8 ). shows that DO-ptGQD show more effect in reversing the outcome of scopolamine as compared with the ptGQD and donepezil.

In this study graphene quantum dots was successfully synthesized and then functionalized with the donepezil hydrochloride. Quantum dots prepared with the root extract of Polygala tenuifolia and then functionalized with donepezil hydrochloride was the effective drug delivery system for the cure of alzheimer disease and due to the lesser size they can simply cross BBB and also provides sustained delivery of donepezil hydrochloride. SEM and TEM study before and after the functionalization of drug shows that the particle were spherical in shape with size below than 100 nm. Behavioural models show that the formulation DO-ptGQD improves the learning and memory ability of rats. Formulation DO-ptGQD significantly inhibits the AchE, increase the protein and glutathione level and decrease the nitric oxide and lipid peroxide level. Thus synthesis of DO-ptGQD is safe and effective approach for the treatment of alzheimer disease.

KT: Investigation, Methodology, Software, Writing –original draft, Writing –review & editing. PS: Data curation, Formal analysis, Software. RS: Data curation, Software, Validation, Visualization VD: Conceptualization, Investigation, Project administration, Resources, Software, Supervision, Visualization. SJ: Data curation, Methodology, Writing –review & editing. SS: Investigation, Supervision, Validation, Visualization. All authors have read and agreed to the published version of the manuscript.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: Authors would like to thank Department of Pharmacy, Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan for providing all essential equipments for investigation of sample.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A graphene quantum dot photodynamic therapy agent with high singlet oxygen generation

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: J. Ge, M. Lan, B. Zhou, W. Liu, L. Guo, H. Wang, Q. Jia, G. Niu, X. Huang, H. Zhou, A Graphene Quantum Dot Photodynamic Therapy Agent with High Singlet Oxygen Generation, Nat. Commun. 5 (2014) 1-8.
